title: Elekmania picardae
taxonomy: Elekmania picardae belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family of plants includes over 2,800 species and is one of the largest in the world. Elekmania picardae is a genus within the family and consists of a single species, making it a relatively rare and unique plant. The classification of Elekmania picardae places it within the order Malpighiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The plant's classification is part of the broader taxonomy of flowering plants, which continues to be studied and refined by botanists.
